candidates.evidence_quote: led by comparable sales growth at the Cheesecake Factory restaurants of 2.4% versus the prior year and 12.6% versus 2019
traffic at the Cheesecake Factory meaningfully outperformed the broader casual dining industry
So specifically, pricing was at 9.5. Mixed was a negative 6.1, and then traffic was a negative 1.0.
Compared to last year, just slightly less alcohol attachment, maybe a little bit less appetizer attachment
we expect effective commodity inflation of low single digits for Q4 as our broad market basket continues to stabilize

none of our restaurants will be included in that wage mandate, but obviously there could be some ripple effect
And with improved restaurant staffing levels, our best-in-class operators have been able to increase their focus on consistently executing our strategy.
Our industry-leading retention rates are now running above pre-pandemic levels.
Our internally measured net promoter score metrics across both the dine-in and off-premise are consistently exceeding pre-pandemic levels.
The Cheesecake Factory off-premise sales for the third quarter totaled 21% of sales, just below second quarter levels
early demand continues to exceed our internal expectations and member satisfaction scores are over-indexing
North Italia third quarter comparable sales increased a solid 8% from the prior year and 28% versus 2019.
Forewall margins for the adjusted mature North Italia locations was 12.5%, down from 15.4% in the previous quarter.
North Italia margins were impacted by seasonally lower sales and higher utility costs, which were exacerbated by record high temperatures in the southwest
We now have nine locations open, averaging over $200,000 per week so far this year.
Flower Child sales totaled $32.2 million, up 11% from the prior year, and sales per operating week were $80,000.
And external bakery sales were $17.4 million during the third quarter of fiscal 2023.
with our desserts driving the strong affinity for the Cheesecake Factory brand, as illustrated by our industry-leading dessert sales
last week we announced plans to develop our third bakery production facility in Charlestown, Indiana
we now expect to open as many as 16 new restaurants in 2023 and four to six new restaurants in the first quarter of 2024
most of the issues today are more around permitting. and dealing with local municipalities and their pace is not what it once was

We now expect two Cheesecake Factory restaurants to open internationally under licensing agreements in 2023.
we anticipate total revenues to be between $870 and $890 million
we anticipate net income margin to be about 4.25% at the midpoint of the sales range
we now anticipate approximately 150 to $160 million in capex to support this year's and some of next year's unit development

returned just over $13.1 million to shareholders via our dividend

notes: Chunk contains a single event: Q3 FY2023 earnings call transcript part 1 of 2 (prepared remarks + early Q&A); no fiscal_kpis in this chunk.

Skipped (vague/minor/folded, 9): seasonality (bare category), school-calendar shift (vague), southwest record heat (folded into utility_costs evidence), medical insurance expense (minor, folded into labor_costs), supply-chain equipment delays (described as abated/historical), consumer spending normalization (covered by casual_dining_demand and menu_mix), overtime/training costs (folded into labor_costs), liquidity/debt balances (no causal claim), alcohol/appetizer attachment (folded into menu_mix).
revenue_surprise is grounded in reported revenue finishing below the company's own guidance range (no consensus figure stated in this part).
Brand-level drivers (north_italia_*, culinary_dropout_*, flower_child_*) kept separate from company-wide metrics per ontology R9.
